dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won the day because Claude Code was the tool commenters kept using as the benchmark that actually worked: it was praised for handling coding tasks that frustrated Codex, and the Microsoft thread treated its spread inside the company as evidence that Anthropic had focused on developer productivity, outweighing one cautionary thread about risky overtrust in finance-model conversions [E2][E4][E3].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the strongest mix of positive sentiment and breadth of discussion. Across three separate front-page stories, Claude Code was described as faster and more reliable than Codex on real work, and its adoption inside Microsoft was framed as a sign that employees preferred it over Microsoft's own AI products. A separate thread did introduce a meaningful caution about using Claude Code for unverified financial-model conversion, but that negative signal was narrower than the two stronger positive drivers [E2][E4][E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex App drew genuine curiosity and one user said Codex had recently been impressively useful, but the dominant reaction was that the product felt slow, disobedient, outdated, and weaker than Claude Code or Gemini on real coding tasks [E1][E8].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code kept showing up as the coding assistant people actually wanted to use: in the Codex thread it was praised for one-shotting work that frustrated users, and the Microsoft thread treated its internal spread as proof of momentum, even as another thread warned that using it to convert complex financial models without deep review was unsafe [E2][E4][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini 3 Flash earned repeated coder praise for feeling smart, fast, and cheap enough to iterate with, but those endorsements came as comparison comments inside OpenAI and Anthropic/Microsoft discussions rather than from Gemini-led stories of its own [E6][E7].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The Microsoft thread focused on Copilot sprawl, muddled naming, weak Office/M365 results, and the sense that Microsoft squandered GitHub Copilot's early lead while employees reached for Claude Code instead [E5].

evidence.summary: In the Codex App launch thread, a detailed top comment called Codex slow, poor at following instructions, outdated, and materially worse than Claude Code and Gemini on actual coding work.
The Codex App discussion repeatedly used Claude Code as the favorable comparison point, with one commenter saying Claude Code one-shotted tasks that Codex fumbled.

In 'Two kinds of AI users are emerging,' commenters reacted skeptically to claims that Claude Code could almost one-shot a 30-sheet Excel financial model into Python, warning that critical logic would likely be wrong and unverified.

The 'Claude Code is suddenly everywhere inside Microsoft' thread framed Anthropic as having built the tool people inside Microsoft actually wanted, with comments explicitly saying Anthropic had pulled ahead by focusing on real productivity.

The same Microsoft thread heavily criticized Copilot's confusing product naming, poor Office results, lack of convincing dogfooding, and Microsoft's failure to capitalize on GitHub Copilot's early position.
In the Codex App thread, a commenter said Gemini 3 Flash was smarter, faster to iterate with, and had better common sense for coding than Codex.
Inside the Microsoft/Claude Code thread, a direct comparison praised Gemini 3 Flash as standing alongside top coding models while being smaller, faster, and cheaper.
The Codex App thread also contained some real enthusiasm, including a commenter saying Codex had recently been shockingly good, though they still ran into launch and documentation issues immediately.
